title: Tidal Energy Harvest
intent: Ocean tidal energy infrastructure using resonance amplification, adapting to lunar cycles and coastal turbulence to harvest predictable, renewable power.

noise_to_insight.◆→: Shipping lane interference reframed as co-design opportunity — tidal arrays become navigational landmarks
noise_to_insight.ᘯᘰ: Storm chaos reframed as peak energy windows — extreme flows yield maximum harvest
noise_to_insight.⊗≡: Site variability reframed as design diversity — each coast demands unique geometry
noise_to_insight.⊗: Frame-dependence reframed as local adaptation — place-tuned resonance replaces universal design

insight: An ocean engine that breathes with the moon, where storms become peak harvest, shipping conflicts become co-design, and every coastline demands its own resonant geometry. The tide does not deliver uniform energy — it delivers a conversation between gravity, geography, and time, and the infrastructure must learn to listen.
